# Scott Curtner

> Technology Audit Manager (VP) and GRC leader with 30+ years
> of experience at Wells Fargo and Bank of America. CIA®, CISA®,
> ITIL® 4 certified. Pursuing AAIA™ (Advanced in AI Audit).
> Based in San Francisco Bay Area.

Scott Curtner is a technology auditor who came up through the
machines — server administration, enterprise mobility, and
product management before moving into IT audit and GRC at VP
level. He writes about AI tools, audit practice, real estate
investing, and the intersection of technical depth and
business judgment.

## Professional

- [scottcurtner.com](https://www.scottcurtner.com/): Full
professional profile — experience, credentials, testimonials,
and writing.

## Writing

- [My AI Quietly Rewired My Memory System — and I Had to Catch Up](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/rewired-memory/): I asked my AI for my task list and got a single entry. Here's how my 2nd brain quietly rerouted tasks from database to wiki — and why I ratified it.
- [I Don't Publish Articles. I Release Them Through an AI Control.](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/publish-control/): A missing canonical tag on a Sunday morning pulled a thread. By end of day I'd built a two-agent AI publish control for my blog. Here's the system.
- [Google Released a Standard for AI Knowledge Bases. I Adopted It — Then Extended It.](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/okf-freshness/): Google's OKF gives AI agents a clean way to read your knowledge. It says nothing about whether that knowledge is still true. Here's the layer I built on top.
- [When Does AI Use Become Shadow AI?](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/shadow-ai/): Where personal AI productivity tools cross into governance risk — and how dependency triggers the line.
- [Integrative vs. Disintegrative Pain in Technical Learning](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/integrative-pain/integrative-pain-blog-post.html): Applying Mark Divine's pain framework to learning hard technical skills.
- [I Walked Through a Vacant Rental With My Phone](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/unit-a/): How an MCP memory server turns voice notes and phone photos into organized action.
- [My 2nd Brain Got a Wiki — and Writes Itself](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/2nd-brain-wiki/): Adding a compiled wiki layer to an AI memory system backed by GitHub and Claude.
- [From My Grandfather's Ledger to the Plaid API](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/grandpaledger-to-plaid-api/): Replacing 30 years of paper real estate bookkeeping with a Python/Plaid system.
- [I Built My Daughter an AI Brain for College](https://www.scottcurtner.com/articles/clarice-2ndbrain/): Building a persistent AI memory system for a college-bound kid using Open Brain MCP.
- [Letting My AI Edit Its Own Memory](https://www.scottcurtner.com/article-memory-edit.html): Extending Open Brain MCP with update and delete tools — and why a write-only memory system is a control weakness.
- [How I Built a Professional Website for Free](https://www.scottcurtner.com/article-1.html): GitHub Pages, Claude, and the DNS gotcha at Network Solutions nobody warns you about.
- [Honored by San Bruno](https://www.scottcurtner.com/article-2.html): Receiving the 2026 City of San Bruno Community Recognition Award after 13+ years with the San Bruno Education Foundation.

## Learning

- [Agentic Operations](https://www.scottcurtner.com/learning/agentic-operations/): A hands-on, four-level course on building AI agents — from "the context window is the model's entire world" to owning SLOs for a live agent in production. Written and published as I go.

Professional network profile and activity.
